---
title: "Methods"
---

The full methodology is detailed in the PRISMA-P 2015 protocol
(`protocol/PRISMA-P_protocol.md`) and the PROSPERO registration
(`protocol/PROSPERO_registration.md`). This page summarises the highlights.

## Eligibility

Adults (≥18 years) with atopic dermatitis defined by Hanifin-Rajka, UK Working
Party criteria, ICD-10 L20, or validated self-report; vs adults without AD.
Outcomes: depression, anxiety, suicidality / self-harm — each pooled
separately.

## Searches

PubMed and Embase queries (`search/pubmed_query.txt`,
`search/embase_query.txt`) re-run monthly. Trial registers and grey-literature
sources searched at each formal annual update. PRESS-2015 peer review on file.

## Risk of bias

ROBINS-E for non-randomised studies; RoB 2 for any contributing RCT. Two
independent reviewers; consensus or third-reviewer adjudication.

## Quantitative synthesis

- **Random-effects** with REML τ² estimation.
- **Hartung-Knapp-Sidik-Jonkman** confidence intervals.
- Effect harmonised to OR; HR/RR-only studies sensitivity-tested.
- Heterogeneity: τ², I², 95 % prediction interval.
- **Subgroup**: age stratum, AD severity, sex, region, ascertainment, design.
- **Meta-regression** for mean age and % female (k ≥ 10).
- **Sensitivity**: leave-one-out, low-RoB-only, adjusted-only, incident-only,
  OR-native-only.
- **Publication bias**: funnel plot + Egger (k ≥ 10) + trim-and-fill.

## Certainty

GRADE per outcome. The pipeline produces a *draft* judgement using
pre-specified rules; the review team finalises after discussion.
